PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-13-2022 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-13-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrea G. Van Leesten for and with Nathaniel Johnston, Jr., and Verna Shaheen, in court, Petitioners; Gene Benitez, via Zoom, for and with Diane Lee Bos, Petitioner.

The court confirms with Ms. Bos that she has resided at the 1188 Mission Street address since before the inception of this matter. The court advises that it is inclined to appoint Ms. Bos as she has priority over Nathaniel Johnston, Jr. The issue of whether there are any conditions on Ms. Bos being bonded is discussed. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to their individual petitions. The court reminds counsel of its obligation to follow the probate code and reiterates that it is appointing Ms. Bos and Verna Shaheen and Nathaniel Jr's petitions are denied accordingly.

After additional, brief discussion, the court appoints Diane Bos Administrator of the Estate with full authority. Bond is set at $1,245,000. Amy Harrington is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Dec-26-2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Limited Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-10-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Andrea Van Leesten, via Zoom, for Nathaniel Johnston, Jr., Petitioner; Gene Benitez, via Zoom, for Diane Bos, Petitioner.

The court queries counsel regarding whether they have met and conferred. Ms. Van Leesten advises that she is willing to work with opposing counsel and reports of attempts to contact him regarding her client and Ms. Bos acting as co-Administrators but there has been no response. Mr. Benitez acknowledges receipt of requests to act as co-Administrators, but his client is not agreeable to the idea as Ms. Van Leesten's client lives in Florida which would likely mean that his client would be performing most of the administrative duties. The court and counsel discuss the bond issue. The court advises that it will likely order a full bond and will not be ordering a minimum bond for the out-of-state Administrator or for Ms. Bos and states that Ms. Bos cannot be bonded for the full amount. Ms. Van Leesten reports that her client is bondable for the full amount. The court advises that it is not inclined to appoint an out-of-state Administrator and asks if the parties are amenable to the appointment of a third-party. Ms. Van Leesen advises that she is amenable to the court's inclination. The court advises that it intends to deny Diane Bos' petition and continue Nathaniel Johnston, Jr.'s petition. The court instructs counsel to meet and confer regarding a third-party fiduciary, preferably one that is local, and that if the parties cannot agree, the court will consider granting Nathaniel Johnston, Jr.'s petition but require a full bond. The court reminds Ms. Van Leestesn that she needs to file an amended petition, or file a new petition, to appoint a neutral. Ms. Van Leesten advises that she intends to file proof that her client is fully bondable.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
